<!-- Auto-generated by codebase-harness -->

# Gotchas And Limitations

## Markdown Is The Runtime

There is no compiler or test suite for the repository content. Verification is
mostly structural: path existence, JSON validity, shell syntax for harness
scripts, wrapper parity, and evidence quality in incident documents.

## Parallel Task State

Do not use `.ai/runtime/current-task.json` for authority. It is a legacy
singleton and remains only to avoid deleting another agent's old state. New
sessions publish `.ai/runtime/current-task/{session_id}.json`.

## Task ID Allocation

Do not list `.ai/tasks/` and choose the next number manually. Always allocate
with `.ai/harness/scripts/claim-task-id.sh <slug>` so concurrent agents cannot
collide.

## Cursor Rule Residue

Some `.cursor/rules/*.mdc` files predate the centralized wrapper model and lack
`<!-- Centralized rule: ... -->` markers. Do not silently delete or rewrite
them; either migrate them into `.ai/harness/rules/` deliberately or leave them
as legacy user-authored Cursor rules.
